dal.title: Dal Makhani
dal.desc: A timeless Punjabi delicacy, Dal Makhani is slow-simmered to perfection with butter and cream, creating a velvety texture and bold flavors. Best enjoyed with naan or rice for a truly satisfying experience.
dal.author: Arpita Sharma
method: Soak whole black lentils (urad dal) and kidney beans (rajma) overnight in water.
Drain and pressure cook them with fresh water and a little salt until soft and fully cooked.
Heat butter and oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute.
Add tomato puree, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Cook until the oil separates.
Add the cooked dal and rajma along with some cooking water. Mix well.
Let it simmer on low heat for 20–30 minutes for rich flavor.
Add fresh cream and garam masala, then mix gently.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with naan or rice.

paneer.title: Paneer Butter Masala
paneer.desc: Creamy tomato-based curry with soft paneer cubes.
paneer.author: Aman
method: Heat butter in a pan and sauté finely chopped onions until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute until fragrant.
Add tomato puree along with red chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Cook until the oil separates.
Add paneer cubes and mix gently so they don’t break.
Pour in fresh cream and mix well to create a rich gravy.
Let it simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and mix lightly.
Serve hot with naan or rice.

Gobi.title: Aloo Gobi
Gobi.desc: Fragrant rice flavored with cumin seeds.
Gobi.author: Sayma
method: 2 potatoes (peeled and cubed)
1 cauliflower (cut into florets)
1 large onion (finely chopped)
2 tomatoes (chopped)
1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
2 tablespoons oil
1 teaspoon cumin seeds
1 teaspoon turmeric powder
1 teaspoon red chili powder
1 teaspoon garam masala
Salt to taste
Fresh coriander leaves (for garnish)

Fried.title: Veg Fried Rice
Fried.desc: Comfort food made with kidney beans curry and rice.
Fried.author: Khushi
method: Soak chickpeas overnight and pressure cook until soft.
Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds, letting them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute.
Add tomato puree along with spices and cook until oil separates.
Add cooked chickpeas and mix well with the masala.
Let it simmer on low heat for 15–20 minutes.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with bhature.

Rajma.title: Rajma Chawal
Rajma.desc: Traditional Indian bread cooked in a tandoor.
Rajma.author: Arpita
method: Soak rajma overnight and pressure cook until soft.
Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute.
Add tomato puree and spices, cooking until oil separates.
Add the cooked rajma along with some water and mix well.
Let it simmer on low heat for 15–20 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and mix gently.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

mushroom.title: Rasmalai
mushroom.desc: Soft sweet balls soaked in flavored milk.
mushroom.author: Sanjana Kumari
method: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds, letting them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute until fragrant.
Add tomato puree along with spices and cook until oil separates.
Add sliced mushrooms and mix well.
Cook until mushrooms release water and become tender.
Add fresh cream and mix gently for a rich texture.
Sprinkle garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

malai.title: Rasmalai
malai.desc: Soft sweet balls soaked in flavored milk.
malai.author: Sanjana Kumari
method: Mix grated paneer, mashed potatoes, cornflour, and salt to form a dough.
Shape into small balls (koftas) and deep fry until golden brown.
Heat butter in a pan and sauté chopped onions until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute.
Add tomato puree and spices and cook until oil separates.
Add a little water and let the gravy simmer.
Add fresh cream and mix well for a rich texture.
Add fried koftas just before serving.
Serve hot with naan or rice.
